text: 1. Subcontractors engaged in "Hot work" which means riveting, welding, flame cutting or other fire or sparkproducing operation must have a fire extinguisher in the area in which they are working. This operation in an unenclosed area or operational building also requires a 'fire watch' to ensure that fires will not be created. Fire Watch will be required during and 60 minutes after the operation is complete, and 120 minutes for all work on the roof. (See hot work permit for further guidance.) Drums and containers which contain or have contained flammable or combustible liquids shall be kept closed. Empty containers shall be removed from the hot work area.
